message:
feat(views): add NodeView.get(n, default=None) across all graph types

NetworkX's NodeView supports a dict-like `get()` lookup: `G.nodes.get(n)`
returns the attribute dict for `n` if present, or `default` otherwise.
The Rust-backed NodeView classes (Graph, DiGraph, MultiGraph, MultiDiGraph)
previously only exposed `__getitem__`, raising KeyError for missing nodes.
Any Python caller doing `G.nodes.get(n, fallback)` was broken.

Adds a `get(n, default=None)` method to all four NodeView implementations
in fnx-python:

  - crates/fnx-python/src/views.rs          (Graph / NodeView)
  - crates/fnx-python/src/digraph.rs        (DiGraph / DiNodeView,
                                             MultiDiGraph / MultiDiGraphNodeView)
  - crates/fnx-python/src/lib.rs            (MultiGraph / MultiGraphNodeView)

Semantics mirror dict.get:
  * Canonicalizes the key via node_key_to_string (same as __getitem__)
  * Returns the attribute dict if the node exists
  * Returns default (or None if omitted) otherwise
  * Never raises KeyError

This matches nx.classes.reportviews.NodeView.get and unblocks generator
parity tests that rely on `G.nodes.get(...)` fallbacks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
